volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 5.2.871 - Section 5.4.950)

sections.label: Section 5.2.871
sections.original: At nunc dehinc spero aeternam inter nos gratiam
sections.translation: But now , from henceforth , I hope , Thais , that there will be lasting good-will between us . Many a time , from some affair of this kind and from a bad beginning , great friendships have sprung up . What if some Divinity has willed this ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.875
sections.original: Equidem pol in eam partem accipioque et volo .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , for my own part I both take it in that view and wish to do so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.876
sections.original: Immo ita quaeso . Unum hoc scito ; contumeliae
sections.translation: Yes , prithee , do so . Be sure of this one thing , that I did not do it for the sake of affronting you , but in consequence of passion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.877b
sections.translation: I understand , and , i ’ faith , for that reason do I now the more readily forgive you . I am not , Chaerea , of a disposition so ungentle , or so inexperienced ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.880
sections.original: Neque ita imperita ut quid amor valeat nesciam .
sections.translation: as not to know what is the power of love .

sections.label: Section 5.2.881
sections.original: Te quoque iam , Thais , ita me Di bene ament , amo .
sections.translation: So may the Deities kindly bless me , Thais ; I am now smitten with you as well .

sections.label: Section 5.2.882
sections.original: Tum pol tibi ab istoc , hera , cavendum intelligo .
sections.translation: Then , i ’ faith , mistress , I foresee you must have a care of him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.883
sections.translation: I would not dare —

sections.label: Section 5.2.883b
sections.translation: I won ’ t trust you at all in any thing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.883c
sections.translation: Do have done .

sections.label: Section 5.2.884
sections.original: Nunc ego te in hac re mihi oro ut adiutrix sies :
sections.translation: Now I entreat you that you will be my assistant in this affair .

sections.label: Section 5.2.885
sections.original: Ego me tuae commendo et committo fidei ;
sections.translation: I intrust and commit myself to your care ; I take you , Thais , as my protectress ; I implore you ; I shall die if I don ’ t have her for my wife .

sections.label: Section 5.2.888
sections.translation: But if your father should say any thing —

sections.label: Section 5.2.888b
sections.translation: Oh , he ’ ll consent , I ’ m quite sure of that , if she is only a citizen .

sections.label: Section 5.2.889b
sections.translation: If you will wait a little ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.890
sections.original: Si vis , iam frater ipse hic aderit virginis :
sections.translation: the brother himself of the young woman will be here presently ; he has gone to fetch the nurse , who brought her up when a little child ; you yourself , shall be present Chaerea , at his recognition of her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.893
sections.translation: I certainly will stay .

sections.label: Section 5.2.893b
sections.translation: In the mean time , until he comes , would you prefer that we should wait for him in the house , rather than here before the door ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.895
sections.translation: Why yes , I should like it much .

sections.label: Section 5.2.895b
sections.original: Quam tu rem actura obsecro es ?
sections.translation: Prithee , what are you going to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.896
sections.translation: Why , what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.896b
sections.original: Rogitas ? hunc tu in aedes cogitas
sections.translation: Do you ask ? Do you think of admitting him after this into your house ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.897b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.897c
sections.translation: Trust my word for it , he ’ ll be creating some new disturbance .

sections.label: Section 5.2.898b
sections.translation: O dear , prithee , do hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.2.899
sections.translation: You seem to me to be far from sensible of his assurance .

sections.label: Section 5.2.900
sections.translation: I ’ ll not do any thing , Pythias .

sections.label: Section 5.2.900b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I don ’ t believe you , Chaerea , except in case you are not trusted .

sections.label: Section 5.2.901b
sections.translation: Nay but , Pythias , do you be my keeper .

sections.label: Section 5.2.902b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I would neither venture to give any thing to you to keep , nor to keep you myself : away with you !

sections.label: Section 5.2.904
sections.translation: Most opportunely the brother himself is coming .

sections.label: Section 5.2.904b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m undone . Prithee ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.905
sections.original: Abeamus intro Thais : nolo me in via
sections.translation: let ’ s be gone in-doors , Thais . I don ’ t want him to see me in the street with this dress on .

sections.label: Section 5.2.906b
sections.translation: For what reason , pray ? Because you are ashamed ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.907
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.907b
sections.translation: Just so ? But the young woman —

sections.label: Section 5.2.907c
sections.translation: Go first ; I ’ ll follow . You stay here , Pythias , that you may show Chremes in .

sections.label: Section 5.3.909
sections.original: Quid ? quid venire in mentem nunc possit mihi ?
sections.translation: Well ! what now can suggest itself to my mind ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.910
sections.original: Quidnam qui referam illi sacrilego gratiam
sections.translation: What , I wonder , in order that I may repay the favor to that villain who palmed this fellow off upon us ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.911b
sections.translation: Really , do bestir yourself more quickly , nurse .

sections.label: Section 5.3.912b
sections.translation: I am bestirring .

sections.label: Section 5.3.912c
sections.translation: So I see ; but you don ’ t stir forward .

sections.label: Section 5.3.913
sections.translation: Have you yet shown the tokens to the nurse ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.913b
sections.translation: All of them .

sections.label: Section 5.3.914
sections.translation: Prithee , what does she say ? Does she recognize them ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.914b
sections.translation: Yes , with a full recollection of them .

sections.label: Section 5.3.915
sections.original: Bene edepol narras ; nam illi faveo virgini .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , you do bring good news ; for I really wish well to this young woman . Go in-doors : my mistress has been for some time expecting you at home . ( CHREMES and SOPHRONA go into THAIS ’ s house .) But look , yonder I espy that worthy fellow , Parmeno , coming : just see , for heaven ’ s sake , how leisurely he moves along . I hope I have it in my power to torment him after my own fashion .

sections.label: Section 5.3.920
sections.original: Ibo intro de cognitione ut certum sciam :
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in-doors , that I may know for certain about the discovery ; afterward I ’ ll come out , and give this villain a terrible fright .

sections.label: Section 5.4.922
sections.original: Reviso quidnam Chaerea hic rerum gerat .
sections.translation: I ’ ve just come back to see what Chaerea has been doing here . If he has managed the affair with dexterity , ye Gods , by our trust in you , how great and genuine applause will Parmeno obtain !

sections.label: Section 5.4.925
sections.original: Nam ut mittam , quod ei amorem difficillimum et
sections.translation: For not to mention that a passion , full of difficulty and expense , with which he was smitten for a virgin , belonging to an extortionate courtesan , I ’ ve found means of satisfying for him , without molestation , without outlay , and without cost ; then , this other point — that is really a thing that I consider my crowning merit ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.930
sections.translation: to have found out the way by which a young man may be enabled to learn the dispositions and manners of courtesans , so that by knowing them betimes , he may detest them ever after . ( PYTHIAS enters from the house unperceived .) For while they are out of doors , nothing seems more cleanly , nothing more neat or more elegant ;

sections.label: Section 5.4.935
sections.original: Quae , cum amatore suo quum coenant , liguriunt .
sections.translation: and when they dine with a gallant , they pick daintily about : [*] to see the filth , the dirtiness , the neediness of these women ; how sluttish they are when at home , and how greedy after victuals ; in what a fashion they devour the black bread with yesterday ’ s broth :— to know all this , is salvation to a young man .

sections.label: Section 5.4.940
sections.original: Ego pol te pro istis dictis et factis , scelus ,
sections.translation: Upon my faith , you villain , I ’ ll take vengeance upon you for these sayings and doings ; so that you sha ’ n ’ t make sport of us with impunity . ( Aloud , coming forward .) O , by our trust in the Gods , what a disgraceful action ! O hapless young man ! O wicked Parmeno , to have brought him here !

sections.label: Section 5.4.943b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.944
sections.original: Miseret me : itaque ut ne viderem misera huc effigi foras
sections.translation: I do pity him ; and so that I mightn ’ t see it , wretched creature that I am , I hurried away out of doors .

sections.label: Section 5.4.945
sections.original: Quae futura exempla dicunt in eum indigna .
sections.translation: What a dreadful example they talk of making him !

sections.label: Section 5.4.945b
sections.translation: O Jupiter ! What is this tumult ? Am I then undone ? I ’ ll accost her . What ’ s all this , Pythias ? What are you saying ? An example made of whom ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.947b
sections.translation: Do you ask the question , you most audacious fellow ? You ’ ve proved the ruin of the young man whom you brought hither for the Eunuch , while you were trying to put a trick upon us .

sections.label: Section 5.4.949b
sections.original: Quid ita ? aut quid factum est ? cedo .
sections.translation: How so , or what has happened ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.950
sections.original: Dicam . Virginem istam Thaidi quae hodie dono data est
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you : that young woman who was to-day made a present to Thais , are you aware that she is a citizen of this place , and that her brother is a person of very high rank ?
